data.company: Department of Computer Science, University of South Dakota
details: Design deep learning architectures that are intrinsically explainable and adversarially robust, embedding transparency and security directly into model design.
Build and benchmark end-to-end experimental pipelines on real-world datasets to validate trustworthy AI systems through explainability and adversarial robustness evaluation.
Support undergraduate students in math for machine learning, Python, and JavaScript through lab sessions, and evaluate assignments to provide constructive feedback.
Contributed to the design and development of IBFD's Tax Research Platform, collaborating with international teams.
Improved platform accessibility in compliance with WCAG 2.2 AA standards.
Worked closely with back-end developers to design and develop RESTful APIs for front-end integration.
Led junior developers, ensured end-to-end application maintenance, and conducted R&D for feature enhancements.
Designed and developed three Angular-based client applications, including two for a financial platform.
Collaborated with back-end engineers to design and integrate APIs for seamless front-end functionality.
Supported multiple live projects by fixing bugs, optimizing code, and implementing new features.
Mentored junior developers on front-end technologies and software development best practices.
Collaborated with cross-functional software engineering teams to develop new features and resolve existing system issues.
Led the development of key functionalities, including audio/video calling, dental module integration, medicine suggestion settings, and dynamic prescription design.
Conducted research and feasibility analysis to identify optimal implementation strategies for upcoming features.
Evaluated and integrated tools, libraries, APIs, and frameworks to enhance performance and ensure security in a data-intensive web environment.
